column3: to finish doing ~
column5: 彼女は小説を書き上げた。
column6: She finished writing her novel.

column3: so much… that
column5: 嬉しさのあまり、彼女は涙を流しました。
column6: She was so happy that she cried.

column3: too much; so much… that; excessively ~
column5: 最近あまりに暑すぎる。
column6: It's too hot recently..

column3: do something together
column5: 私たちはその件について話し合いましょう。
column6: Let's discuss together regarding that matter.

column3: should; can; it’d be good if ~
column5: どうすればいいですか？
column6: What should I do?

column3: should have; would have been better if ~
column5: そうすればよかった。
column6: I should have done that.

column3: the more… the more ~
column5: ビートルズの音楽は聞けば聞くほど好きになります。
column6: The more I listen to The Beetles; the more I end up liking their music.

column3: would have; should have; if only ~
column5: 映画はとても面白かった。君も行けばよかったのに。
column6: The movie was very interesting. You should have gone!

column3: only; just ~ (negative description)
column5: 夏は暑いばかりで楽しくないですよ。
column6: Summer is just hot all the time; it's not enjoyable.

column3: not only... but also; as well as ~
column5: このマンションは狭いばかりでなく、暗いです。
column6: This apartment is not only very small; it's also quite dark.

column3: should do; must do ~
column5: 私はそれについてもっと知るべきだ。
column6: I should know more about that.

column3: should not do; must not do ~
column5: そんなことをすべきではない。
column6: You shouldn't do such a thing.

column3: not really; not particularly
column5: それは別に悪いことでもない。
column6: That's not particularly a bad thing.

column3: for the first time in (period of time)
column5: 今日、やく2週間ぶりに雨が降った。
column6: Today; it rained for the first time in 2 weeks.

column3: currently; during; at some point; throughout; before the end of ~
column5: 私は一晩中泣いていた。
column6: I cried all night.

column3: as much as ~
column5: 食べたいだけ食べなさい。
column6: Eat as much as you like.

column3: not only… but also ~
column5: ここのレストランはどれも安いだけでなく、美味しい。
column6: Not only is everything cheap at this restaurant; it is all delicious.

column3: but; however; although; regarding ~
column5: スキー行きたいんだけど、お金が全然ないよ。
column6: I want to go skiing; but I have no money.

column3: full of; covered with; a lot of (something undesirable)
column5: この公園は、猫だらけだ。
column6: There are cats all over this park.

column3: no matter how (much)
column5: どんなに頑張っても、70点しか取れなかった。
column6: No matter how hard I tried; I never got more than 70 points.

column3: no matter what; at any cost; after all ~
column5: 私はどうしても自分の家がほしい。
column6: I really want to have my own house (to the point I'd do anything).

column3: to pretend; to act as if ~
column5: 彼は聞こえないふりをした。
column6: He pretended to not hear.

column3: suddenly; accidentally; unexpectedly; unintentionally ~
column5: ふと街で彼に会った。
column6: I met him in the street by chance.

column3: tend to; tendency to; frequently; often; to do something easily
column5: 私は、仕事でミスしがちです。
column6: I tend to make a lot of mistakes at work.

column3: very difficult to; hard to ~
column5: 信じがたいことだが、これは事実だ。
column6: This may be hard to believe; but this is the truth.

column3: -like; -looking; -looked; tending to ~
column5: 彼は人の名前を忘れ気味である。
column6: He tends to forget people's names.

column3: each; every; at intervals of ~
column5: バスは１０分ごとに通っている。
column6: The buses run every 10 minutes.

column3: degree; extent; bounds; upper limit
column5: 君ほど美しい人はいません！
column6: There is no one that rivals your beauty.

column3: is not as… as ~
column5: 彼女ほど優しい人はいない。
column6: There’s no one as kind as her.

column3: all at once
column5: 彼は一度にクッキーを3つ取った。
column6: He took 3 cookies at once.

column3: no matter how ~
column5: これはいくら安くても、買いたくない。
column6: No matter how cheap this is、I don't want to buy it.

column3: more and more; continue to ~
column5: 最近、この町の人口は減る一方だ。
column6: Recently; this town's population continues to decrease.

column3: emphasis; what on earth; what in the world
column5: 一体どこで彼に会ったんだ。
column6: Where on earth did you meet him?

column3: maybe; most likely; confirmation of information; express surprise towards listener
column5: トムさんも来るじゃない？
column6: Tom will surely come as well?

column3: or something ~
column5: 彼は病気か何かだろう。
column6: He must be sick or something.

column3: half-; not yet finished; in the middle of~
column5: お風呂に入りかけたときに電話が鳴った。
column6: The phone rang when I was about to get in the bath.

column3: through; from [A] to [B]
column5: 東京から京都にかけて新幹線で行くのは何時間かかるの？
column6: How long does it take to get from Tokyo to Kyoto by bullet train?

column3: instead of; as a substitute for​; in exchange for; in return for
column5: 私が部長の代わりに出張に行きます。
column6: I will go on a business trip on behalf of my manager.

column3: as a result of; after ~
column5: 彼は仕事をサボった結果として首になった。
column6: As a result of him skipping work; he was fired.

column3: after all; eventually; in the end ~
column5: 結局彼はそれを買わなかった。
column6: He didn't buy it in the end.

column1: 決して～ない（けっして～ない）
column3: never; by no means ~
column5: あなたのことは決して忘れません。
column6: I could never forget you.

column3: unable to do; too much to finish/complete
column5: この部屋に100人は入り切れない。
column6: This room cannot fit 100 people.

column3: only; just; since; after ~
column5: 私は一人きりで暮らすのが嫌だ。
column6: I don't want to live all alone.

column3: to do something completely to the end
column5: どうしてそう言い切れるのですか。
column6: How can you say that?

column3: a casual suffix to confirm something you are trying to remember
column5: 次のテストっていつだっけ？
column6: When is the next test again?

column3: to do something for a long time or to move something inside
column5: この紙に必要な情報を書き込んでください。
column6: Please write the necessary information on this paper.

column3: for sure (emphasize preceding word); precisely; definitely ~
column5: これこそ望むところです。
column6: This is the very thing I want.

column3: (must) do
column5: 大声を出さないこと。
column6: Don't speak with a loud voice.

column3: from the fact that ~
column5: 道路が濡れていることから、雨が降ったということが分かる。
column6: Considering the ground is wet; I know that it must have rained.

column3: expresses a standard; rule or expectation
column5: この図書館では2週間まで本が借りられることになっています。
column6: At this library; you can borrow books for up to 2 weeks.

column3: there is no need to; (something) never happens; there is no possibility that ~
column5: 謝ることはないよ。
column6: There is no need for you to apologize

column3: although; but ~
column5: パーティーは行けることは行けるが、たぶん1時間くらい遅刻する。
column6: I can go to the party but I will probably be about 1 hour late.

column3: approximately; about; around; to the extent ~
column5: どれくらい日本に住んでいますか？
column6: How long have you been living in Japan?

column3: and yet; though; in spite of; despite ~
column5: 背が高いくせに早く走れない。
column6: Despite being tall; he/she cannot run fast.

column3: as if; as though; just like ~
column5: 合格した！まるで夢のようだ。
column6: I passed! It’s like a dream!

column3: there's no way; that's impossible; you must be joking
column5: まさかそうなるとは思わなかった。
column6: That's the last thing I expected.

column3: hardly; rarely; seldom
column5: 彼はめったに本を読まない。
column6: He rarely reads books.

column3: and; also; as well; either/or; neither/nor
column5: 本を読むのが好きな人もいれば、嫌いな人もいます。
column6: If there are people who like to read books; there will also be people who dislike it.

column3: perhaps; maybe; perchance; by any chance ~
column5: もしかしたら、明日行けないかもしれない。
column6: I might not be able to come tomorrow.

column3: if; in the case; supposing ~
column5: もしも明日は晴れたらピクニックに行きましょう。
column6: If it's sunny tomorrow let's go out for a picnic.

column3: intended for; aimed at ~
column5: 初心者向けのコースがありますか。
column6: Do you have a course for beginners?

column3: suitable for ~
column5: この本は初心者向きである。
column6: This book is suitable for beginners.

column3: rather; instead; better ~
column5: 私はむしろ一人で行きたい。
column6: I would rather go alone.

column3: but; although; despite ~
column5: 狭いながらも、このマンションは大好きだ。
column6: Despite it being small I love this apartment.

column3: can do; is not impossible to ~
column5: 走れば間に合わないことはないよ。急ごう！
column6: We can still make it if we run. Let’s hurry up!

column3: must do; unless/if you don't ~
column5: もう行かないと...
column6: I have to go now...

column3: very; quite; pretty; rather; just not ~
column5: なかなか面白そうです。
column6: That sounds quite interesting.

column1: なんか / なんて / など
column3: give examples; show modesty; make light of ~
column5: 試験も近いし、テレビなど見ている暇はない。
column6: Exams are close and I don't have time to do things like watch TV.

column3: to do something again; to do over
column5: この作文はミスが多いから、書き直したほうがいいよ。
column6: This essay has a lot of mistakes; so you should do a rewrite.

column3: as much as possible
column5: なるべく気をつけてください。
column6: Be as careful as you can!

column3: because; the reason is; if you want to know why
column5: なぜなら彼は病気だったからです。
column6: The reason is because he was sick.

column3: I hear that; heard that ~
column5: 彼、彼女ができたんだって。
column6: I heard he got a girlfriend.

column1: に違いない（にちがいない）
column3: I’m sure/ certain; no doubt that ~
against; contrary to; in contrast to ~
column5: 彼の話は事実に反する。
column6: What he has said is contrary to the facts.

column3: over (a period); through; concerning; regarding ~
column5: テニスにかけては、彼に勝てる人はいない。
column6: There is no one who can beat him in tennis.

column1: に関する / に関して（にかんする / にかんして）
column2: ni kan suru / ni kan shite
column3: about; regarding; related to ~
column5: それに関しては彼は初心者だ。
column6: He is a beginner in regards to that.

column1: にかわって / にかわり
column3: instead of; replacing; on behalf of ~
column5: 弟は、母にかわってご飯を作った。
column6: My little brother made dinner instead of my mom.

column3: compared to; in comparison to ~
column5: 昨日に比べて、今日は少し寒いね。
column6: It's a bit cold today compared to yesterday.

column3: to be used to something
column5: 私は日本の生活に慣れるまでが大変だった。
column6: It was tough before I got used to life in Japan.

column1: において / における
column3: in; at (place); regarding; as for; in relation to ~
column5: 私は全てにおいて頑張ります。
column6: I do my best with everything.

column3: as; therefore; in accordance with; according to; consequently ~
column5: 暗くなるにしたがって、だんだん寒くなってきました。
column6: It began to get colder as it got darker.

column3: even if; even though; regardless of ~
column5: どちらにしても賛成できない。
column6: In either case I cannot agree.

column3: for; considering it’s (something or someone)
column5: それは痛いに違いない。
column6: I bet that hurts.

column3: towards; against; regarding; in contrast with​ ~
column5: 彼は女性に対しては優しい。
column6: He is kind towards her.

column3: to; for; concerning; as far as ... is concerned; regarding ~
column5: 私にとって、漢字の勉強はとても楽しい。
column6: For me; studying kanji is a lot of fun!

column3: concerning; regarding; about; on ~
column5: この問題についてどう思いますか。
column6: What do you think about this problem?

column3: as; in proportion to; with; as… then ~
column5: 台風が近づくにつれて、どんどん風が強くなってきた。
column6: As the typhoon came closer; the wind gradually got stronger.

column3: for the purpose of; in order to ~
column5: 日本語が上手になるには、どうしたらいいですか。
column6: What should I do to improve my Japanese?

column3: according to ~
column5: 天気予報によると、明日は雨だそうです。
column6: According to the weather report; tomorrow it looks like it will rain.

column3: by means of; due to; owing to; because of ~
column5: それは場合による。
column6: That depends on the situation.

column3: ask a question in a polite way
column5: 明日は晴れるのでしょうか。
column6: I wonder if it will be clear tomorrow.

column1: を中心に（をちゅうしんに）
column3: focused on; centered on
column5: 文法を中心に、日本語を勉強する。
column6: To study Japanese with a focus on grammar.

column3: for example; starting with ~
column5: 東京には東京タワーをはじめ多くの観光地がある。
column6: In Tokyo; there are many tourist attractions like Tokyo Tower and so on.

column3: filled with; full of
column5: 彼女に愛を込めて手紙を書きました。
column6: I wrote her a letter full of my love.

column1: を通じて / を通して（をつうじて / をとおして）
column3: through; via; throughout; by ~
column5: そのことは新聞を通じて知った。
column6: I learned that from a newspaper.

column3: thanks to; owing to; because of ~
column5: 彼女のおかげで、私は毎日幸せだ。
column6: Thanks to her; I am happy everyday.

column3: leaving (something) on; leaving (something) still in use​
column5: このドアはいつも開けっぱなしだ。
column6: This door is always left open.

column3: seems like; somewhat; -ish; easily does; often does ~
column5: 油っぽい食事は好きじゃない。
column6: I don't like oily foods.

column3: even; so much as; not even
column5: そんなこと、子供でさえ知っている。
column6: Even children know that.

column3: if only; as long as ~
column5: お金さえあれば幸せです。
column6: I'm happy as long as I have money.

column3: when; at the time of; in the case of ~
column5: あなたが日本へ来る際には連絡ください。
column6: Please contact me if you come to Japan.

column3: while; during; in the middle of ~
column5: 食事の最中に大きな地震が起こった。
column6: During dinner there was a big earthquake.

column3: furthermore; again; more and more ~
column5: 彼は父親よりさらに背が高い。
column6: He is even taller than his father.

column3: conjunction: well; now; then ~
column5: さてどうしよう。
column6: What shall I do now?

column3: because of; due to; as a result of ~
column5: さっきコーヒーを飲んだせいで、なかなか寝られません。
column6: Because I drank coffee a little while ago; I can't seem to fall asleep.

column3: at the most; at best; to the utmost
column5: せいぜい頑張ろうね！
column6: Let's give it our all!

column3: for a moment; for a while; for the time being
column5: しばらくここで待てください。
column6: Please wait here for a while.

column3: have no choice but ~
column5: やるしかない。
column6: You just gotta do it.

column3: hence; for that reason; because of ~
column5: あなたは明日、そのための時間がありますか？
column6: Do you have time tomorrow for that?

column3: or; or else ~
column5:
歩こうか、それとも車で行こうか？,Shall we walk or drive?
そうもない /そうにない,sou mo nai /sou ni nai,very unlikely to; showing no signs of ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%9d%e3%81%86%e3%82%82%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84-sou-mo-nai-%e3%81%9d%e3%81%86%e3%81%ab%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84-sou-ni-nai-meaning/,先生、明日までに宿題ができそうにないんですが、もう少し待っていただけませんか。,Teacher; I won't be able to finish my homework by tomorrow; could I possibly get an extension?
すでに,sude ni,something has already been done/taken place; is already the case,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%99%e3%81%a7%e3%81%ab-sude-ni-meaning/,彼らはすでに外出してしまいました。,They already went out.
すなわち,sunawachi,in other words; namely ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e5%8d%b3%e3%81%a1-%e3%81%99%e3%81%aa%e3%82%8f%e3%81%a1-sunawachi-meaning/,妹の娘、すなわち、めいは、近所に住んでいるのでよく遊びに来ます。,My younger sister's daughter; in other words my niece; lives nearby so I often visit.
数量 + は（すうりょう + は）,suuryou + wa,at least ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e6%95%b0%e9%87%8f-%e3%81%af-suuryou-wa-meaning/,今日はゆっくり休むと言ったけど、１時間は仕事をしました。,I said I would rest today; but I still worked for at least an hour or so.
たものだ,ta mono da,used to do; would often do,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%9f%e3%82%82%e3%81%ae%e3%81%a0-ta-mono-da-meaning/,子どものころはよく川で遊んだものだ。,When I was a child; I often played at the river.
たとたん,ta totan,as soon as; just as ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%9f%e3%81%a8%e3%81%9f%e3%82%93-ta-totan-meaning/,家を出たとたん、雨が降ってきた。,It started raining after I left my house.
たびに,tabi ni,whenever; every time ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%9f%e3%81%b3%e3%81%ab-tabi-ni-meaning/,この曲を聞くたびに、家族を思い出す。,Every time I hear this song; it reminds me of my family.
ために,tame ni,for; in order to; for the benefit of; because of; as a result of ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%9f%e3%82%81%e3%81%ab-tame-ni-meaning/,日本へ旅行をするために、貯金しています。,I'm saving money to travel to Japan.
確かに（たしかに）,tashika ni,surely; certainly ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e7%a2%ba%e3%81%8b%e3%81%ab-tashika-ni-meaning/,それは確かにそのように見えます。,That certainly looks that way.
たて,tate,just done; freshly done; newly done,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%9f%e3%81%a6-tate-meaning/,焼きたてのパンは美味しいよ。ちょっと食べてみる？,Freshly baked bread is delicious. Do you want to try some?
たとえ～ても,tatoe~temo,even if… is the case,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%9f%e3%81%a8%e3%81%88%ef%bd%9e%e3%81%a6%e3%82%82-tatoe-temo-meaning/,たとえ彼は忙しくても来るよ。,Even if he is busy; he will come.
例えば（たとえば）,tatoeba,for example; for instance ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e4%be%8b%e3%81%88%e3%81%b0-%e3%81%9f%e3%81%a8%e3%81%88%e3%81%b0-tatoeba-meaning/,日本の食べ物は美味しいです。例えば、すしはすごく美味しい！,Japanese food is delicious; for example; sushi is so delicious!
たって,tatte,even if; even though; no matter how ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%9f%e3%81%a3%e3%81%a6-tatte-meaning/,タクシーに乗ったって間に合わないよ。,Even if you take a taxi; you won't make it in time.
てばかりいる,te bakari iru,only; nothing but ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%81%b0%e3%81%8b%e3%82%8a%e3%81%84%e3%82%8b-te-bakari-iru-meaning/,この猫は寝てばかりいます。,This cat does nothing but sleep.
てごらん,te goran,(please) try to; (please) look,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%81%94%e3%82%89%e3%82%93-te-goran-meaning/,もう一度やってごらん。,Try it one more time.
てはじめて,te hajimete,not until; only after [x] did I,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%81%af%e3%81%98%e3%82%81%e3%81%a6-te-hajimete-meaning/,日本に来てはじめてラーメンを食べた。,I had never eaten ramen before coming to Japan.
てからでないと,te kara de nai to,must first do; cannot do without first doing ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%81%8b%e3%82%89%e3%81%a7%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84%e3%81%a8-te-kara-de-nai-to-meaning/,親の許可をもらってからでなければ、日本への留学が出来ない。,You must first get your parent's permission; otherwise you will not be able to study abroad in Japan.
てしょうがない・てしかたがない,te shou ga nai/te shikata ga nai,can't help but; very; extremely ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%81%97%e3%82%87%e3%81%86%e3%81%8c%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84-te-shou-ga-nai-%e3%81%a6%e4%bb%95%e6%96%b9%e3%81%8c%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84-shikata-ga-nai-meaning/,私はそれが気になってしょうがない。,I can't help thinking about that.
て済む（てすむ）,te sumu,sufficient by; no problem to; resolve by ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e6%b8%88%e3%82%80-te-sumu-meaning/,その夜は何の事故もなくて済んだ。,The night passed without any accidents.
てはいけないから,te wa ikenai kara,in order to not~; to prevent a negative situation,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%81%af%e3%81%84%e3%81%91%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84%e3%81%8b%e3%82%89-te-wa-ikenai-kara-meaning/,雨が降るといけないから傘を持って行きなさい。,It would be no good if it rains; so please take an umbrella with you.
ている場合じゃない（ているばあいじゃない）,teiru baai janai,this is no time to be doing ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%81%84%e3%82%8b%e5%a0%b4%e5%90%88%e3%81%98%e3%82%83%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84-teiru-baai-janai-meaning/,今は笑っている場合ではない。,This is not an occasion for laughter.
的（てき）,teki,change noun into na-adjective or adverb,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e7%9a%84-%e3%81%a6%e3%81%8d-teki-meaning/,もっと具体的なアイディアを出してください。,Please give more specific ideas.
ても始まらない（てもはじまらない）,temo hajimaranai,even if you... it’s no use; there is no point to ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%82%82%e5%a7%8b%e3%81%be%e3%82%89%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84-temo-hajimaranai-meaning/,今さらそんなことを言っても始まらない。,It is no use talking about that now.
ても構わない（てもかまわない）,temo kamawanai,it doesn't matter if ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%82%82%e3%81%8b%e3%81%be%e3%82%8f%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84-temo-kamawanai-meaning/,雨が降っても構わない。,I don't mind if it rains.
てもしょうがない / てもしかたがない,temo shou ga nai / temo shikata ga nai,there's no point to~; it's no use to~; it cannot be helped,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a6%e3%82%82%e3%81%97%e3%82%87%e3%81%86%e3%81%8c%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84%e3%83%bb%e3%81%a6%e3%82%82%e4%bb%95%e6%96%b9%e3%81%8c%e3%81%aa%e3%81%84-temo-shou-ga-nai-temo-shikata-ga-nai-meaning/,,
と言えば（といえば）,to ieba,speaking of; when you talk of; when you say; if it were the case that ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a8%e8%a8%80%e3%81%88%e3%81%b0-%e3%81%a8%e3%81%84%e3%81%88%e3%81%b0-to-ieba-meaning/,,
といい / たらいい,to ii / tara ii,it would be nice if; should; I hope ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a8%e3%81%84%e3%81%84-to-ii-%e3%81%9f%e3%82%89%e3%81%84%e3%81%84-tara-ii-meaning/,あなたが気にいるといいな。,I hope you like it.
といっても,to ittemo,although I say; although one might say ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a8%e3%81%84%e3%81%a3%e3%81%a6%e3%82%82-to-ittemo-meaning/,今週は忙しい、といっても先週ほどじゃない。,I’m busy this week; but not as much as last week.
ということだ,to iu koto da,I heard; it means that; in other words ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a8%e3%81%84%e3%81%86%e3%81%93%e3%81%a8%e3%81%a0-to-iu-koto-da-%e3%81%a8%e3%81%ae%e3%81%93%e3%81%a8%e3%81%a0-to-no-koto-da-meaning/,ニュースによると、明日は雨が降るということだ。,I heard on the news that it will rain tomorrow.
というのは,to iu nowa,this means; the meaning of … is; because; that is to say ~,https://jlptsensei.com/learn-japanese-grammar/%e3%81%a8%e3%81%84%e3%81%86%e3%81%ae%e3%81%af-to-iu-nowa-meaning/,「コンビニ」というのはコンビニエンスストアのことだ。,"konbini"" means ""convenience store""."

column3: speaking of; when you talk of; when you say ~
column5: ベトナムと言えば、バイクが多いですね。
column6: Speaking of Vietnam there are a lot of bikes.

column3: rather than ~
column5: 彼はかっこいいというよりかわいい。
column6: He is more cute than cool.

column1: とみえる / とみえて
column3: it seems that ~
column5: 彼は病気だとみえる。
column6: He appears to be sick.

column1: とすれば・としたら・とすると
column2: to sureba / to shitara / to suru to
column3: in the case of~; assuming~; if A then B
column5: 選ぶとすれば、僕はこれだな。
column6: If I were to choose; I would go with this.

column3: together with; at the same time as; as well as ~
column5: 私の人生は、ギターと共にある。
column6: I spend my life together with my guitar.

column1: 途中で/途中に（とちゅうで/とちゅうに）
column3: on the way; in the middle of ~
column5: ごはんを食べている途中に電話がかかってきた。
column6: The phone rang when I was in the middle of eating (dinner).

column3: by the way ~
column5: ところで、今どこにいるの？
column6: By the way; where are you now?

column3: even so; however; even though ~
column5: 今日は晴れだと思っている。ところが、雨だ。
column6: I thought today would be sunny. However; it is raining..

column3: in the same way as; in the way; as ~
column5: 彼は言われたとおりにした。
column6: He did as he was told.

column3: to do until the end; to continually do; through ~
column5: やると決めたことは最後までやり通すべきだ。
column6: When you decide to do something; you should see it through to the end.

column3: as; in the role of ~
column5: 彼は教師として働いている。
column6: He has been working as a teacher.

column3: cannot possibly be; hardly ~
column5: そんなこと、私にはとても出来ない。
column6: There's no way I could do something like that.

column1: とは限らない（とはかぎらない）
column3: not necessarily so; is not always true
column5: 先生の答えがいつも正しいとは限りません。
column6: The teacher's answers are not always correct.

column3: accidentally; unintentionally; by mistake
column5: 料理がおいしくてつい食べ過ぎた。
column6: The food was so good that I ate too much.

column3: finally; at last; in the end ~
column5: ついに着いたのだ！
column6: I finally arrived!

column3: while; incidentally; at the same time; on the way ~
column5: 買い物のついでに、本屋に寄りました。
column6: I stopped by a book store while shopping.

column3: in other words; in summary; in short ~
column5: 彼は獣医、つまり、動物の医者です。
column6: He’s a veterinarian. In other words he’s an animal doctor.

column3: I thought I~; I believe I~; was planning to~; was intending to ~
column5: 言ったつもりだった。
column6: I thought I said it.

column3: with the intention of doing ~
column5: 冗談のつもりで言ったのです。
column6: I meant it to be a joke.

column3: while; before ~
column5: 明るいうちに、帰りましょう。
column6: Let's head home while it's still light outside.

column3: upon; after; when; for; in order to
column5: 外国語を勉強する上で辞書はなくてはならないものだ。
column6: In order to study a foreign language; you need to have a dictionary.

column3: as well; besides; in addition to; not only… but also ~
column5: 今日は曇っている上に風が強いので、とても寒く感じられる。
column6: Today in addition to being cloudy; the wind is strong and so it feels very cold.

column1: は別として（はべつとして）
column3: aside from; apart from; except for; whether or not ~
column5: 彼女の料理は見た目は別として、味はとても美味しい。
column6: Apart from the appearance her food tastes very good.

column3: not to mention; not only ... but also ~
column5: 彼女は、勉強はもちろん料理もよくできる。
column6: Not only is she a good student; but she is also good at cooking.

column1: は～で有名（は～でゆうめい）
column3: famous for ~
column5: この学校はフットボールが強いことで有名です。
column6: This school is famous for being strong in football.

column3: for that reason; no wonder; as you’d expect; the fact is; so you mean ~
column5: 毎日ギターを弾くから上手に弾けるわけだ。
column6: I'm good at playing guitar; because I play every day.

column3: it doesn’t mean that; it is not the case that; I don’t mean that; it is not true that ~
column5: 料理が作れないわけではないが、忙しいからあまり作らない。
column6: It's not that I can't cook. I'm just busy and don't do it that often.

column3: there is no way that; it is impossible to ~
column5: 彼はお金がないですから、家を買えるわけがない。
column6: He has no money; there's no way he can buy a house.

column3: must not; cannot afford to; must; have no choice but to ~
column5: 明日はテストがあるから、ゲームをするわけにはいかない。
column6: I have a test tomorrow; so there's no way I can play games right now.

column3: considering ~; for ~; despite ~
column5: 彼は年のわりに元気だ。
column6: He is in good shape for his age.

column3: on purpose; intentionally ~
column5: 彼はわざと間違えた。
column6: He made a mistake on purpose.

column3: to go to the trouble of; to go out of one's way to ~
column5: 私が困っているとき、彼女はわざわざ助けてくれた。
column6: She went out of her way to help me when I was in trouble.

column3: in comparison to; rather than; more than ~
column5: 兄は私よりも大きい。
column6: My older brother is bigger than I am.

column3: there is no way to; it’s impossible to ~
column5: どうにもしようがない。
column6: Nothing can be done about it.

column1: ような気がする（ようなきがする）
column3: have a feeling that; feels like; seems like; think that ~
column5: それが本当のような気がする。
column6: I feel that it is true.

column3: in order to; so that ~
column5: バスに乗り遅れないように早く出ました。
column6: I left early so I would not be late for the bus.

column1: ように見える（ようにみえる）
column3: to look; to seem; to appear ~
column5: 彼は元気なように見える。
column6: He looks very energetic.

column3: not try to; not make an effort to ~
column5: 彼は早く寝ようとしない。
column6: He doesn't try to go to bed early.

column3: try to; attempt to; be about to ~
column5: ドアを開けようとしたが、鍵がかかっていました。
column6: I tried opening the door; but it was locked.

column3: without doing ~
column5: 勉強せずに試験に受かった。
column6: I passed the exam without studying.

column3: can’t help but feel; can’t help but do; must do ~
column5: あの本を読まずにはいられない。
column6: I can't help but to read that book.

column3: apiece; each; at a time
column5: 私たちは一歩ずつ進みましょう。
column6: Let's move forward one step at a time.
